question_html:
Change the voice.

(a) Please post the letter

(b) Mr. Sulayman teaches us English grammar.

(c) Who was seen by you in the morning?

(d) I watched him clean his house.

(e) The police have apprehended the suspect in connection with the robbery.
solution_html:
(a) You are requested to post the letter/ Let the letter be kindly posted.

(b) We are taught English grammar by Mr. Sulayman/ English grammar is taught to us by Mr. Sulayman.

(c) Whom did you see in the morning?

(d) I watched his house being cleaned by him/ He was watched cleaning his house by me.

(e) The suspect in connection with the robbery has been apprehended by the police.

Marks Distribution:

(0.5× 5) = 2.5 marks for changing the voice.

[N.B: 0.5 mark will be deducted for one or two (spelling or grammatical) mistakes and 01 mark for three or more (spelling or grammatical) mistakes.]

question_html: Man is born free but everywhere he is in chains- please explain this statement in your own words.
solution_html:
The sentence “Man is born free, but everywhere he is in chains” means that every person enters the world with natural freedom, equal rights, and the ability to choose. However, as life goes on, people become bound by laws, customs, and social inequalities that limit their liberty. It shows the contrast between natural freedom and the restrictions created by society and civilization. Many of these “chains” are the result of injustice, unfair rules, and exploitation. True freedom is possible only when people work together for justice, equality, and collective well-being, ensuring dignity for everyone.

Marks Distribution:

01 mark for identifying the central contrast between the innate freedom of birth vs. the constraints imposed by society.

01 mark for correctly and clearly explaining what the "chains" represent & listing "laws, customs, social inequalities, injustice, unfair rules, and exploitation."

0.5 mark for writing a clear conclusion statement about true freedom.

[N.B: 0.5 mark will be deducted for one or two (spelling or grammatical) mistakes and 01 mark for three or more (spelling or grammatical) mistakes.]
